name: opentf-toolkit-nightly

metadata.name: opentf-toolkit-nightly
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License

documentation:
# opentf-toolkit

This package is part of the OpenTestFactory initiative.

## Overview

This package contains libraries and schemas to facilitate the creation of
plugins for the OpenTestFactory orchestrator.

The package documentation is part of the
[OpenTestFactory orchestrator](https://opentestfactory.org/impl/index.html)
documentation, in the "Create plugins" section.


## License

Copyright 2021-2023 Henix, henix.fr

Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
You may obtain a copy of the License at

    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0

Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
limitations under the License.

functions.name: opentf.toolkit.core.register_defaultplugin
documentation:
Register default plugin.

The core helpers assume a plugin context is defined.  If no plugin
is found while searching the frames, the registered default plugin
is used.

functions.name: opentf.toolkit.core.deregister_defaultplugin
documentation: Deregister default plugin.

functions.name: opentf.toolkit.core._getlocal
documentation: Local helpers

functions.name: opentf.toolkit.core.publish_event
documentation: Publish event.
# Publication helpers

functions.name: opentf.toolkit.core.publish_error
documentation: Publish ExecutionError event.

functions.name: opentf.toolkit.core.publish_providerresult
documentation: Publish ProviderResult event.

functions.name: opentf.toolkit.core.publish_generatorresult
documentation: Publish GeneratorResult event.

functions.name: opentf.toolkit.core.debug
documentation: Log debug message.

functions.name: opentf.toolkit.core.info
documentation: Log info message.

functions.name: opentf.toolkit.core.warning
documentation: Log warning message.

functions.name: opentf.toolkit.core.error
documentation: Log error message.

functions.name: opentf.toolkit.core.fatal
documentation: Log error message and exit with error code 2.

functions.name: opentf.toolkit.core.fail
documentation: Raise an ExecutionError event.

functions.name: opentf.toolkit.core.runner_on_windows
documentation: Check if execution environment is windows-based.

functions.name: opentf.toolkit.core.normalize_path
documentation:
Normalize path.

If the execution environment is windows-based, forward slashes will
be replaced by backward slashes.

If spaces are found in path, it will be surrounded by double quotes.

functions.name: opentf.toolkit.core.join_path
documentation:
Join two paths.

If the execution environment is windows-based, forward slashes will
be replaced by backward slashes.

No attempt is made to ensure operation makes sense.

If `path2` is None, returns `path1`.

functions.name: opentf.toolkit.core.set_secret
documentation:
Set secret.

The secret will be hidden in warning/debug/error outputs.
# Steps

functions.name: opentf.toolkit.core.export_variable
documentation:
Export variable.

The variable will be visible by following steps.

functions.name: opentf.toolkit.core.export_variables
documentation:
Export variables.

`args` are dictionaries containing variable names and values as
key/value pairs.  Items in `args` can be None.

Returns a list of steps.

functions.name: opentf.toolkit.core.validate_params_inputs
documentation: Validate inputs for providers param actions.

functions.name: opentf.toolkit.core.attach_file
documentation:
Attach file.

`path` is a relative file path (from the current directory)

`kwargs` are key/value pairs that are added as workflow command
parameters:

```python
core.attach_file('/foo/bar.xml',type='text/xml', charset='utf8')
# gives ::attach type=text/xml, charset=utf-8::/foo/bar.xml

functions.name: opentf.toolkit.core.delete_file
documentation: Delete file.

functions.name: opentf.toolkit.core.delete_directory
documentation: Delete directory.

functions.name: opentf.toolkit.core.touch_file
documentation: Touch file.

functions.name: opentf.toolkit.core.create_file
documentation: Create file.

classes.name: opentf.toolkit.core.ExecutionError
documentation:
An ExecutionError exception.

Only expected to be raised in a workflow thread.  Will publish
the corresponding ExecutionError event if in this context.
# Exception helpers

functions.name: opentf.commons.selectors.compile_selector
documentation:
Compile selector.

# Required parameters

- exprs: a string, a comma-separated list of expressions

# Optional parameters

- resolve_path: a boolean, default True

# Returned value

A list of tuples, the 'compiled' selectors.

# Raised exceptions

A _ValueError_ exception is raised if at least one expression is
invalid.

functions.name: opentf.commons.selectors._evaluate
documentation: Evaluate whether obj matches selector.

functions.name: opentf.commons.selectors.match_selectors
documentation:
Check if object matches selector.

An empty selector matches.  The selectors can be strings or
compiled selectors.

The complete selector feature has been implemented.  `selector` is
of form:

    expr[,expr]*

where `expr` is one of `key`, `!key`, or `key op value`, with
`op` being one of `=`, `==`, or `!=`.  The
`key in (value[, value...])` and `key notin (value[, value...])`
set-based requirements are also implemented.

# Required parameters

- obj: a dictionary

# Optional parameters

- fieldselector: a string or a list of opcodes or None
- labelselector: a string or a list of opcodes or None

# Returned value

A boolean.

# Raised exceptions

A _ValueError_ exception is raised if `fieldselector` or
`labelselector` is not a valid.

functions.name: opentf.commons.selectors.prepare_selectors
documentation:
Prepare selectors if defined.

`src` is typically a request arg dictionary.  The selectors,
`fieldSelector` and `labelSelector`, are compiled if defined.

# Required parameters

- src: a dictionary-like structure

# Returned value

A pair of lists of opcodes or None.

# Raised exceptions

A _ValueError_ exception is raised if the selectors defined in `src`
are not a valid.
.

functions.name: opentf.toolkit._one_and_only_one
documentation: Check that one and only one argument is not None.
Helpers for provider plugins

functions.name: opentf.toolkit._normalize_inputs
documentation:
Normalize inputs.

The 'normalized' form for inputs is with `-` separators, not `_`.

Non-normalized inputs are removed from the dictionary.

# Raised exceptions

A _core.ExecutionError_ is raised if an input is provided twice, in
a normalized as well as a non-normalized form.

functions.name: opentf.toolkit._get_pcv
documentation: Extract prefix, category, version from labels.

functions.name: opentf.toolkit._maybe_get_item
documentation: Get most relevant item from cache if it exists.

functions.name: opentf.toolkit._ensure_inputs_match
documentation:
Check inputs.

Normalize inputs, fills missing optional inputs with their default
values if specified.

# Raised exceptions

A _core.ExecutionError_ is raised if a required entry is missing,
or if an unexpected entry is found.

functions.name: opentf.toolkit._get_target
documentation:
Find target for labels.

Finds the most specific provider.  Returns None if no provider
matches.

`prefix/category[@vn]` is more specific than `category[@vn]`.

`category@vn` is more specific than `category`.

`category[@vn]` is more specific than `prefix`.

functions.name: opentf.toolkit._read_hooks_definition
documentation:
Read hooks definition file.

Try to read hooks definition file and set or replace existing hooks
with those of `hooksfile`.

# Required parameters

- plugin: a Flask object
- hooksfile: a string, the hooks definition file
- schema: a string, the schema name
- invalid: a dictionary, a hook definition

functions.name: opentf.toolkit._dispatch_providercommand
documentation:
Provider plugin dispatcher.

`handler` is expected to return either a list of steps or raise a
_core.ExecutionError_ exception.
Dispatchers

functions.name: opentf.toolkit._dispatch_executioncommand
documentation: Channel plugin dispatcher.

functions.name: opentf.toolkit._dispatch_generatorcommand
documentation: Generator plugin dispatcher.

functions.name: opentf.toolkit._run_handlers
documentation: Run file handlers.
Watchdog

functions.name: opentf.toolkit._watchdog
documentation: Watch changes and call handlers when appropriate.

functions.name: opentf.toolkit._start_watchdog
documentation: Set up a watchdog that monitors specified files for changes.

functions.name: opentf.toolkit.watch_file
documentation:
Watch file changes.

There can be more than one handler watching a given file.  A handler
is a function taking at least two parameters: a `plugin` object and
a file path (a string).  It may take additional parameters.  It will
be called whenever the file changes.

# Required parameters

- plugin: a Flask application
- path: a string, the file path
- handler: a function

# Optional parameters

- *args: an array
- **kwargs: a dictionary

The provided extra parameters, if any, are passed to the handler
whenever it is called.

functions.name: opentf.toolkit.watch_and_notify
documentation:
Watch statuses changes in items.

Check item status change at regular interval, call notify if
changes detected.

# Required parameters

- plugin: a Flask application
- status: a function taking an item and returning a boolean
- items: an iterable
- notify: a function of no arguments

functions.name: opentf.toolkit._subscribe
documentation: Subscribe for the relevant event.

functions.name: opentf.toolkit.run_plugin
documentation:
Start and run plugin.

Subscribe to the relevant events before startup and tries to
unsubscribe in case of errors.

Spurious subscriptions may remain in case of brutal termination.

functions.name: opentf.toolkit.make_plugin
documentation:
Create and return a new plugin service.

One and only one of `channel`, `generator`, `provider`, `providers`,
or `publisher` must be specified.

If no `descriptor` is specified, there must be `plugin.yaml` file in
the same directory as the caller source file.  If none is found the
execution stops.

- Create default config
- Subscribe to eventbus
- Add publication handler
- Create service (not started)

Some 'optional' parameters are required for some plugin types:

`args` is required for channel handlers.  It must be a list of one
element that implements the `__contains__` protocol.

# Required parameters

- name: a string
- description: a string
- `channel`, `generator` or `provider`: a function
- providers: a dictionary

# Optional parameters

- descriptor: a dictionary or a list of dictionaries or None (None
  by default)
- schema: a string or None (None by default)
- configfile: a string or None (None by default)
- args: a list or None (None by default)

# Raised exceptions

A _ValueError_ exception is raised if the provided parameters are
invalid.

# Returned value

A plugin service (not started).

functions.name: opentf.scripts.startup._get_env
documentation: Read var from env, using default if not set.
Environment variables helpers

functions.name: opentf.scripts.startup._get_env_int
documentation: Read var from env, using default if not set or not int.

functions.name: opentf.scripts.startup.dump_environment
documentation: Dump environment variables.

functions.name: opentf.scripts.startup._expand
documentation: Perform path substitution.

functions.name: opentf.scripts.startup._generate_token
documentation: Generate temporary key and JWT token.

functions.name: opentf.scripts.startup.start_microservice
documentation: Start cmd in a detached process, returning popen object.

functions.name: opentf.scripts.startup.parse_and_start
documentation: Lookup item manifests and start them if not disabled.

functions.name: opentf.scripts.startup.write_init_file
documentation: Write service json file.

functions.name: opentf.scripts.startup.get_eventbus_endpoint
documentation: Get eventbus endpoint.

functions.name: opentf.scripts.startup.maybe_start_eventbus
documentation: Start eventbus if needed.

functions.name: opentf.scripts.startup.wait_for_eventbus
documentation:
Wait for eventbus readiness.

If `eventbus` is not defined in `conf`, assumes an external eventbus
is used.

# External eventbus

If the `OPENTF_EVENTBUS_WARMUPURL` environment variable is defined,
use it as the eventbus readiness endpoint.  Otherwise, use the
default value and adapt it using `BUS_HOST` and `BUS_PORT`.

If `BUS_TOKEN` is defined, use it as a bearer token.

functions.name: opentf.scripts.startup.wait_for_observer
documentation: Wait for observer subscription.

functions.name: opentf.scripts.startup.start_services
documentation: Lookup core services and start them.

functions.name: opentf.scripts.startup.start_plugins
documentation: Lookup plugins and start them.

functions.name: opentf.scripts.startup.maybe_generate_token
documentation: Generate token if no trusted keys defined.

functions.name: opentf.scripts.startup.maybe_populate_keystore
documentation: Populate Java keystore if CURL_CA_BUNDLE defined.

functions.name: opentf.scripts.startup.add_keystore_certificate
documentation:
Add certificate to keystore.

!!! warning
    This calls `keytool`, which requires root privileges, as it
    add certificates to the system's keystore.

Certificates will have an alias of the form:

    `opentf:{ca_counter}_{random string}`

# Required parameters

- ca_counter: an integer, the certificate position in the bundle
- ca: the certificate as a string

functions.name: opentf.scripts.startup.check_environment
documentation:
Check environment consistency.

Some variables are inter-dependent, so checking them ease
misconfiguration diagnostics.

functions.name: opentf.scripts.startup.show_version
documentation: Show version info.

functions.name: opentf.scripts.startup.watch
documentation: Watch services and plugins, exiting on failure.

functions.name: opentf.scripts.startup.main
documentation: Starting all services, as defined in ./squashtf.yaml, waiting.
Main

functions.name: opentf.commons.schemas.get_schema
documentation:
Get specified schema.

# Required parameters

- name: a string, the schema name (its kind)

# Returned value

A _schema_.  A schema is a dictionary.

# Raised exceptions

If an error occurs while reading the schema, the initial exception
is logged and raised again.

functions.name: opentf.commons.schemas.validate_schema
documentation:
Return True if instance validates schema.

# Required parameters

- schema: a string, the schema name (its kind)
- instance: a dictionary

# Returned value

A (bool, Optional[str]) pair.  If `instance` is a valid instance of
`schema`, returns `(True, None)`.  If not, returns `(False, error)`.

# Raised exceptions

If an error occurs while reading the schema, the initial exception
is logged and raised again.

functions.name: opentf.commons.pubsub.make_event
documentation:
Return a new event dictionary.

# Required parameters

- schema: a string

# Optional parameters

A series of key=values

# Returned value

A dictionary.

functions.name: opentf.commons.pubsub.make_subscription
documentation:
Generate a subscription manifest.

# Required parameter

- name: a string
- selector: a dictionary or None
- target: a string
- context: a dictionary

# Returned value

A validated _subscription manifest_.

# Raised exceptions

A _ValueError_ exception is raised if the `selector` is not a valid
subscription selector.

functions.name: opentf.commons.pubsub._dispatch_events
documentation: Async event dispatch thread handler.

functions.name: opentf.commons.pubsub.subscribe
documentation:
Subscribe on specified endpoint.

`kind` is of form `[apiVersion/]kind` or None.

# Required parameters

- kind: a string or None
- target: a string
- app: a flask app

# Optional parameters

- labels: a dictionary
- fields: a dictionary
- expressions: a dictionary
- fieldexpressions: a dictionary

# Returned value

A _uuid_ (a string).

# Raised exceptions

Raise a _SystemExit_ exception (with exit code 1) if the
subscription fails.

functions.name: opentf.commons.pubsub.unsubscribe
documentation:
Cancel specified subscription

#  Required parameters

- subscription_id: a string (an uuid)
- app: a flask app

# Returned value

A `requests.Response` object.

functions.name: opentf.commons.pubsub.publish
documentation:
Publish publication on specified endpoint.

If `publication` is a dictionary, and if it has a `metadata` entry,
a `creationTimestamp` sub-entry will be created (or overwritten if
it already exists).

# Required parameters

- publication: an object
- context: a dictionary

# Returned value

A `requests.Response` object.

functions.name: opentf.commons.pubsub.make_dispatchqueue
documentation:
Make an asynchronous dispatch queue.

Handles publication failures by waiting for an increasing delay and
re-attempting publication.

The delay is at most 60 seconds.

# Required parameters

- app: a flask app

# Optional parameters

- fn: a function (`publish` by default)
- stragegy: a dictionary or None (None by default)

# Returned value

A _queue_.  Events pushed to this queue will be published.

functions.name: opentf.toolkit.channels.make_variable_linux
documentation: Prepare variable declaration for linux runners.
# os helpers

functions.name: opentf.toolkit.channels.make_variable_windows
documentation: Prepare variable declaration for windows runners.

functions.name: opentf.toolkit.channels._add_default_variables
documentation: Prepare default variables.

functions.name: opentf.toolkit.channels.mask
documentation: Remove masked values.

functions.name: opentf.toolkit.channels.process_opentf_variables
documentation:
Process OPENTF_VARIABLES attachment if available.

If OPENTF_VARIABLES is in the attachments, move its content to
the variables field of the result.

functions.name: opentf.toolkit.channels.process_upload
documentation:
Process ExecutionResult event containing .metadata.upload flag.

Dispatch attachments between ExecutionResult and WorkflowResult events,
publish WorkflowResult.

functions.name: opentf.toolkit.channels.process_output
documentation:
Process output, filling structures.

`event` is expected to have its metadata enriched with the following
fields:

- channel_os: the runner OS
- channel_temp: the temporary directory on the runner

# Required parameters

- event: an ExecutionCommand event
- resp: an integer
- stdout: a list of strings
- stderr: a list of strings
- jobstate: a JobState object
- _get: a function copying a remote file to a local path
- _put: a function copying a local file to a remote environment

# Returned value

An ExecutionResult event.

# Functions arguments

## get

- destination_url: a string
- remote_path: a string (file location on execution environment)

May raise exceptions.

## put

- remote_path: a string (file location on execution environment)
- source_url: a string

May raise exceptions.

functions.name: opentf.toolkit.channels._make_altshell_script
documentation:
Generate script for non-default shells.

Creates a file in the execution environment and then use the
custom shell to run the script.

# Required parameters

- command: an ExecutionCommand event
- path: a string
- runner_os: a string

# Returned value

A list of two strings: the first is a file creation command, the
second is a command to run the script.

functions.name: opentf.toolkit.channels.make_script
documentation:
Prepare script.

# Required parameters

- command: an ExecutionCommand event
- script_path: a string or None, where to put script on runner
- runner_os: a string

# Returned value

script_path, script_content, script_command.

variables.name: opentf.toolkit.channels.DEFAULT_CHANNEL_LEASE
documentation: how long to keep the offer, in seconds

classes.name: opentf.toolkit.channels.JobState
documentation: # masks helpers

functions.name: opentf.commons._add_securityheaders
documentation: Add DEFAULT_HEADERS to response.
Config Helpers

functions.name: opentf.commons._is_authorizer_required
documentation: Check if ABAC or RBAC is enabled for service.

functions.name: opentf.commons._check_token
documentation:
Check token validity.

Token is checked against known trusted authorities and then against
`token_auth_file`, if any.

The thread-local object `g` is filled with a `payload` entry (the
token payload in JWT mode, `{"sub": "username"}` in ABAC mode) and
possibly a `namespaces` entry (in JWT mode).

# Required parameters

- authz: a string ('bearer xxxxxx')
- context: a dictionary

# Returned value

None if the the bearer token is valid.  A status response if the
token is invalid.

functions.name: opentf.commons._get_contextparameter_spec
documentation:
Get context parameter specification.

Initialize cache if needed, ignoring context parameters specs from
other services.

Adds the `watchdog_polling_delay_seconds` spec.

functions.name: opentf.commons.list_accessible_namespaces
documentation:
Get the accessible namespaces.

If called outside of a request context, returns `['*']`.

# Optional parameters

- resource: a string or None (None by default)
- verb: a string or None (None by default)

# Returned value

A list of _namespaces_ (strings) or `['*']` if all namespaces are
accessible.

functions.name: opentf.commons.can_use_namespace
documentation:
Check if namespace is accessible for current request.

If called outside of a request context, returns True.

# Required parameters

- namespace: a string

# Optional parameters

- resource: a string or None (None by default)
- verb: a string or None (None by default)

# Returned value

A boolean.

functions.name: opentf.commons.authorizer
documentation:
Decorate a function by adding an access control verifier.

# Required parameters

- resource: a string
- verb: a string

# Returned value

The decorated function, unchanged if no authorizer required.

The decorated function, which is expected to be a endpoint, will
reject incoming requests if access control is enabled and the
requester does not have the necessary rights.

functions.name: opentf.commons._make_authenticator
documentation: Make an authenticator function tied to context.

functions.name: opentf.commons.get_actor
documentation:
Get actor.

# Returned value

The subject (user), if authenticated.  None otherwise.

functions.name: opentf.commons.make_app
documentation:
Create a new app.

# Required parameters

- name: a string
- description: a string

# Optional parameters

- configfile: a string or None (None by default)
- schema: a string or None (None by default)
- defaultcontext: a dictionary or None (None by default)
- descriptor: a filename, a dictionary, a list of dictionaries, or
  None (None by default)

# Returned value

A new flask app (not started).  Three entries are added to
`app.config`: `CONFIG`, `CONTEXT`, and `DESCRIPTOR`.

`CONFIG` is a dictionary, the complete config file.  `CONTEXT` is a
subset of `CONFIG`, the current entry in `CONFIG['context']`.  It is
also a dictionary.  `DESCRIPTOR` is the service descriptor.

# Raised Exception

Exits with code error 2 if the context is not found or if the config
file is invalid.

functions.name: opentf.commons.get_context_parameter
documentation:
Get an integer parameter from configuration context.

Exits with an error code of 2 if the parameter is not properly
valued or is not defined.

Resolution order:

- `{app.name.upper()}_{name.upper()}` environment variable
- `{name.upper()}` environment variable (if spec is shared)
- `name` in configuration context
- `spec['default']` if spec defines a default value
- `default` if not None

# Required parameters

- app: a Flask object
- name: a string

# Optional parameters

- validator: a function of two arguments or None (None by default)
- default: any value or None (None by default)

# Returned value

An integer if the parameter has a specification and is expected to
be of type int.  The actual parameter type otherwise.

functions.name: opentf.commons.get_context_service
documentation:
Get service specification from configuration context.

Exits with an error code of 2 if the service is missing.

# Required parameters

- app: a Flask object
- service: a string

# Returned value

A dictionary.

functions.name: opentf.commons.run_app
documentation:
Start the app.

Using waitress as the wsgi server.  The logging service is
configured to only show waitress errors and up messages.

Access logs are only displayed when in DEBUG mode.

functions.name: opentf.commons.make_uuid
documentation: Generate a new uuid as a string.
# Misc. helpers

functions.name: opentf.commons.is_uuid
documentation:
Check if a string is a uuid.

# Required parameters

- uuid: a string

# Returned value

A boolean.

functions.name: opentf.commons.make_status_response
documentation:
Return a new status response object.

# Required parameters

- reason: a non-empty string (must exist in `REASON_STATUS`)
- message: a string

# Optional parameters:

- details: a dictionary or None (None by default)
- silent: a boolean (False by default)

# Returned value

A _flask.Response_.  Its body is a _status_ JSON object.  It has
the following entries:

- kind: a string (`'Status'`)
- apiVersion: a string (`'v1'`)
- metadata: an empty dictionary
- status: a string (either `'Success'` or `'Failure'`)
- message: a string (`message`)
- reason: a string (`reason`)
- details: a dictionary or None (`details`)
- code: an integer (derived from `reason`)
API Server Helpers

functions.name: opentf.commons.annotate_response
documentation:
Annotate response with headers if appropriate.

Handles 'Link' header (for RFC8288) and and `X-Processed-Query`
header (specific, a comma-separated list of query parameter names)

# Required parameters

- response: a Flask Response object

# Optional parameters

- links: a list of strings or None
- processed: a list of strings or None

# Returned value

`response`

functions.name: opentf.commons.validate_pipeline
documentation:
Validate workflow jobs, looking for circular dependencies.

# Required parameters

- workflow: a dictionary

# Returned value

A (`bool`, extra) pair.

If there is a dependency on an non-existing job, returns
`(False, description (a string))`.

If there are circular dependencies in the workflow jobs, returns
`(False, description (a string))`.

If there are no circular dependencies, returns `(True, jobs)` where
`jobs` is an ordered list of job names lists.  Each item in the
returned list is a set of jobs that can run in parallel.
Pipelines Helpers

classes.name: opentf.commons.EventbusLogger
documentation:
A Notification logger.

A logging handler that posts Notifications if the workflow is
known.

Does nothing if the log event is not patched to a workflow.

If `silent` is set to False, will print on stdout whenever it fails
to send notifications.

documentation: Helpers for the OpenTestFactory config.

functions.name: opentf.commons.config.get_named
documentation:
Get an entry from a list of entries.

# Required parameters

- name: a string, the entry 'name'
- entries: a list of dictionaries

# Returned value

A dictionary, the entry with the 'name' `name`.

# Raised exceptions

A _ValueError_ exception is raised if no entry is found or if more
than one entry is found.

functions.name: opentf.commons.config.get_debug_level
documentation:
Get service log level.

Driven by environment variables.  If `{service name}_DEBUG_LEVEL` is
defined, this value is used.  If not, if `DEBUG_LEVEL` is set, then
it is used.  Otherwise, returns `INFO`.

Value must be one of `CRITICAL`, `ERROR`, `WARNING`, `INFO`,
`DEBUG`, `TRACE`, or `NOTSET`.

# Required parameter

- name: a string, the service name

# Returned value

The requested log level if in the allowed values, `INFO` otherwise.

classes.name: opentf.commons.config.ConfigError
documentation: Invalid configuration file.

modules.name: opentf.commons.auth
documentation: Helpers for the OpenTestFactory authn/authz config.
Copyright (c) 2023 Henix, Henix.fr
Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
You may obtain a copy of the License at
http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
limitations under the License.

functions.name: opentf.commons.auth._read_key_files
documentation:
Read a series of key files.

Keys are loaded and stored in the context's `trusted_keys` entry as
a list of `(key value, list of namespaces)` tuples.

Uses `context['authorization_trustedkeys']` to map keys to
namespaces (assuming `default` for keys not present in the
aforementioned entry).

# Required parameters

- items: an iterable of strings
- context: a dictionary

Items are either fully-qualified file names or fully-qualified
directory name ending with '/*'.

For example:

- `/etc/opentf/a_public_key`
- `/etc/opentf/dept_a/*`
- `/etc/opentf/another_public_key`
- `/etc/opentf/dept_b/*`

# Raised exceptions

Raises _ConfigError_ if no key is found.
Config files helpers

functions.name: opentf.commons.auth._read_authorization_policy_file
documentation:
Read ABAC authorization policy file.

Policy file is a JSONL file, of form:

```json
{"apiVersion": "abac.opentestfactory.org/v1alpha1", "kind": "Policy", "spec": {"user": "alice", "namespace": "*", "resource": "*", "apiGroup": "*"}}
...
```

# Required parameters

- file: a string
- context: a dictionary

# Returned value

None.  Fills the `context['authorization_policies']` entry with JSON
policies (dictionaries).

# Raised exceptions

Raises _ConfigError_ if the specified file is not a JSONL file.

functions.name: opentf.commons.auth._read_trustedkeys_auth_file
documentation:
Read trustedkeys file.

Trusted keys auth file is of form:

```text
key,name,"group1,group2,group3","namespace_1,namespace_2"
```

The first 2 columns are required, the remaining columns are
optional.

# Required parameters

- file: a string, the file name
- context: a dictionary

# Returned value

None.  Fills the `context['authorization_trustedkeys'] entry with
tuples, one per non-empty line in file.

# Raised exceptions

Raises _ConfigError_ if the specified token file is invalid.

functions.name: opentf.commons.auth._read_token_auth_file
documentation:
Read token file.

Static token file is of form:

```text
token,user,uid,"group1,group2,group3"
```

The first 3 columns are required, the remaining columns are
optional.

# Required parameters

- file: a string, the file name
- context: a dictionary

# Returned value

Fills the `context['authorization_tokens']` entry with one list per
line in file.

The list contains the token, the user name, the user ID and, if
present, the groups.

# Raised exceptions

Raises _ConfigError_ if the specified token file is invalid.

functions.name: opentf.commons.auth._in_group
documentation:
Check if user is in group.

Group membership is defined in token authorization file.

# Required parameters

- user_id: a string
- group: a string or None
- context: a dictionary

# Returned value

A boolean.  True if the user ID is in the group, False otherwise.
If `group` is None, returns False.

functions.name: opentf.commons.auth._rule_gives_permission
documentation: Check access in namespace.

functions.name: opentf.commons.auth.is_user_authorized
documentation:
Check if resource access is authorized, disregarding namespace.

# Required parameters

- user_id: a string
- resources: a string
- verb: a string
- context: a dictionary

# Returned value

True if `user` is allowed to access resource, False otherwise.

functions.name: opentf.commons.auth.get_user_accessible_namespaces
documentation:
Check access granted by rules.

# Required parameters

- user_id: a string
- context: a dictionary

# Optional parameters

- resource: a string or None (None by default)
- verb: a string or None (None by default)

# Returned value

A list of namespaces or `['*']`.

If `resource` and `verb` are not specified, returns the list of
namespaces for which the user has access to at least one resource
type.

functions.name: opentf.commons.auth.initialize_authn_authz
documentation:
Initialize authn & authz

Handles the following service parameters:

- `--trusted-authorities`
- `--enable-insecure-login`
- `--insecure-bind-address`
- `--authorization-mode` (ABAC, RBAC, JWT)

The `context` is updated accordingly.

# Required parameters

- args: an argparse result
- context: a dictionary

modules.name: opentf.commons.expressions
documentation: Expressions helpers
Copyright (c) 2021, 2022 Henix, Henix.fr
Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
You may obtain a copy of the License at

WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
limitations under the License.

functions.name: opentf.commons.expressions.get_token
documentation:
Get first token in expr.

# Required parameters

- expr: a string

# Returned value

A tuple of three elements: `token`, `kind`, `expr`.

# Raised exceptions

A _ValueError_ exception is raised if the token is invalid.
# Tokenizer

functions.name: opentf.commons.expressions.tokenize
documentation:
Return a list of tokens found in expr.

# Required parameters

- expr: a string

# Returned value

A list of _tokens_.  Each token is a (value, kind) pair.  The list
ends with an `(None, END_TOKEN)` pair.

# Raised exceptions

A _ValueError_ is raised if `expr` contains an invalid token.

functions.name: opentf.commons.expressions._is_infix_operator
documentation: # Operators helpers

functions.name: opentf.commons.expressions.find_path
documentation:
Find longest path in tokens starting at offset start.

A _path_ is an identifier, possibly followed by segments.  Segments
are of the form `.identifier` or `[term]`, where `term` is either a
path or a string.
# Path helpers

functions.name: opentf.commons.expressions.evaluate_path
documentation:
Evaluate path using contexts.

The path is syntactically valid.

If the final part of `path` does not exist in `contexts`, returns
an empty string.

# Raised exceptions

A _ValueError_ exception is raised if `path` does not start with
a context available in `contexts`.

functions.name: opentf.commons.expressions.evaluate_operation
documentation:
Perform binary operation evaluation.

Type casting performed as per specification.

# Required parameters

- lhs: an object
- operator: a string
- rhs: an object

# Returned value

A boolean.

functions.name: opentf.commons.expressions.evaluate_function_arity_1
documentation: # Functions

functions.name: opentf.commons.expressions.evaluate_status_function
documentation:
Evaluate job status function.

Annotates the contexts[SPECIAL_FUNCTIONS] entry with a `__called__`
sub-entry, so that caller can detect status function usage.

functions.name: opentf.commons.expressions._evaluate_identifier
opentf.commons.expressions._evaluate_expression
documentation:
Parse and evaluate expression.

infix = '==' | '!=' | '<=' | '<' | '>' | '>=' | '~='
expression = term ('&&' term | '||' term)*
term = factor (infix factor)?
factor = path | string | number | '!'factor | '('expression')'

# Required parameters

- tokens: a list of tokens
- contexts: a dictionary
- offset: an integer

# Optional parameters

- end_token: a token or a list of tokens (END_TOKEN by default)

# Returned value

A pair.  The first element is an integer, the length of the
evaluated segment (0 if empty).  The second element is an object
(depending on `tokens`).  Could be a boolean, a number, a string, a
list, or a dictionary.

# Raised exceptions

A _ValueError_ exception is raised if the expression is not valid.
In particular, if the end of the expression is reached before
finding an expected end token.

functions.name: opentf.commons.expressions.evaluate
documentation:
Perform expression evaluation, using contexts.

# Required parameters

- expr: a string
- contexts: a dictionary

# Returned value

An object (depending on `expr`).  Could be a boolean, a number, a
string, a list, or a dictionary.

functions.name: opentf.commons.expressions.evaluate_str
documentation:
Perform expression evaluation in string.

`value` is either an expression or a string with expression(s) in
it.

# Required parameters

- value: a string
- contexts: a dictionary

# Returned value

If `value` is an expression, returns its evaluated value (can be
any object).  If `value` contains expressions, returns a string
with the expressions replaced by their values.

functions.name: opentf.commons.expressions.evaluate_item
documentation:
Perform expression evaluation for item.

If `item` is a list or dictionary, perform recursive evaluation.

# Required parameters

- item: any object
- contexts: a dictionary

# Returned value

An object (same type as `item`).

functions.name: opentf.commons.expressions.evaluate_items
documentation:
Perform expression evaluation in items.

If items contain sub-elements, the evaluation is performed recursively.

If the referenced context element does not exist, raises a _KeyError_
exception.

Strip spaces around expressions, but does not strip spaces not in
expressions.

TODO: limit 'variables' context usage ('name', 'with', and 'if')

functions.name: opentf.commons.expressions.evaluate_bool
documentation:
Evaluate expression in context.

`expr` may be surrounded by `${{` and `}}`.

`special_functions`, if provided, is a dictionary of function names
to bools.

# Required parameters

- expr: a string
- contexts: a dictionary

# Optional parameters

- special_functions: a dictionary or None (None by default)

# Returned value

A boolean.

functions.name: opentf.commons.expressions._maybe_remove_expression_syntax
documentation: Strip expression syntax if present.

modules.name: opentf.commons.datasources
documentation: Datasources (testcases, tags and jobs) retrieval helpers

you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.

Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
limitations under the License.

functions.name: opentf.commons.datasources._get_metadata
documentation:
Get metadata of the first workflow event that satisfies filter.

# Required parameters

- filter_: a callable, filtering fuction
- events: a list of events or iterator
- kind_: a string, considered events kind

# Returned value

A possibly empty dictionary, the `.metadata` part of the
first event that satisfies kind and filter conditions.

functions.name: opentf.commons.datasources.parse_testcase_name
documentation:
Parse test case name from testResults notification.

full_name is a string: classname#testcase name

# Returned value

A tuple of two strings: suite and test case name. If one
of strings is empty, returns not empty element value instead.

functions.name: opentf.commons.datasources.in_scope
documentation: Safely evaluate datasource scope.
# Datasource: Testcases

functions.name: opentf.commons.datasources.get_testresults
documentation:
Return a possibly empty list of Notifications.

Each notification in the list is guaranteed to have a
`spec.testResults` entry.

functions.name: opentf.commons.datasources._has_testresult
documentation: Determine if a workflow notification has a testResults element.

functions.name: opentf.commons.datasources._get_workflow_jobs
documentation:
Get workflow jobs that have steps.

TOTO Will have to be reviewed when adding nested generators.

# Required parameters

- events: a list of events

# Returned value

A dictionary.  Keys are job names, values are a (dict, event) pair.

- name: a string, the job's name and the generator's job_id, if any
- job: a dictionary (its `runs-on` entry is a list of strings)
- event: either a workflow or a generatorresult event.

functions.name: opentf.commons.datasources._uses_inception
documentation: Determine if a workflow is the inception workflow.

functions.name: opentf.commons.datasources._get_inception_testresults
documentation:
Get unique testResults notifications for inception workflow.

Note: This is a kludge until we find a reliable way to map such results
to the executed tests list.

functions.name: opentf.commons.datasources._get_testresult_params
documentation:
Get .with.data field of param_step_id.

# Required parameters

- param_step_id: a string
- job: a dictionary

# Returned value

A dictionary, the `.with.data` part of the params step.

# Raised exceptions

An _IndexError_ exception is raised if no params step is found.

functions.name: opentf.commons.datasources._create_testresult_labels
documentation:
Create labels for test result.

# Required parameters

- events: a list, workflow events
- step_origin: a string, the 'execute' step uuid
- exec_step: a dictionary, the 'execute' step
- job_name: a string (the name of the job containing exec_step)
- job: a dictionary, the job containing exec_step
- parent: a dictionary, the event defining the job

# Returned value

A labels dictionary.

functions.name: opentf.commons.datasources._get_testresult_steporigin
documentation:
Find the step that produced the attachment.

# Required parameters

- attachment_origin: a string (the attachment uuid)
- events: a list of events

# Returned value

A step ID (a string) or None.

functions.name: opentf.commons.datasources._get_testresult_labels
documentation:
Get labels for test result.

# Required parameters

- attachment_origin: a string (the attachment uuid)
- events: a list of events

# Returned value

A _labels_ dictionary or None.

functions.name: opentf.commons.datasources.get_testcases
documentation:
Extract metadata for each test result.

Test results are Notification events with a `.spec.testResults`
entry.

# Required parameters

- events: a list of events

# Returned value

A possibly empty dictionary.  Keys are the test result IDs, values
are dictionaries with test case metadata, labels, status, and
execution info.

`testcases` is a dictionary of entries like:

```
apiVersion: testing.opentestfactory.org/v1alpha1
kind: TestCase
metadata:
  name: <<<Test case full name>>>
  id: <<<Test case uuid>>>
  job_id: <<<Test case job uuid>>>
  execution_id: <<<Test case attachment origin uuid>>>
  workflow_id: <<<Test case workflow uuid>>>
  namespace: <<<Test case namespace>>>
  creationTimestamp: <<<Test case provider creation timestamp>>>
test:
  runs-on: <<<Test case execution environment tags>>>
  uses: <<<Test case provider>>>
  technology: <<<Test case technology>>>
  managed: bool <<<True for test referential managed test cases>>>
  job: <<<Test case job name>>>
  test: <<<Test case test reference>>>
  suiteName: <<<Test case suite>>>
  testCaseName: <<<Test case short name>>>
  outcome: <<<success|failure|skipped|error>>>
status: <<<SUCCESS|FAILURE|SKIPPED|ERROR>>>
execution:
  startTime: <<<Test case execution start time>>>
  endTime: <<<Test case execution end time>>>
  duration: <<<Test case execution duration (from result notification)>>>
  errorsList: [<<<Test case general execution errors>>>]
  (failure|warning|error)Details: {<<<Test case failure details>>>}
```

# Raised exceptions

A _ValueError_ exception is raised if there were no test results in
`events` or some scope errors occured retrieving test results.

functions.name: opentf.commons.datasources._make_tag_datasource
documentation: # Datasource: Tags

functions.name: opentf.commons.datasources.get_tags
documentation:
Extract metadata for each execution environment tag.

# Required parameters:

- events: a list of events

# Returned value:

A dictionary. Keys are tags names, values are dictionaries with tag metadata and status.

`tags` is a dictionary of entries like:

```
apiVersion: opentestfactory.org/v1alpha1
kind: Tag
metadata:
  name: <<<Tag name>>>
  workflow_id: <<<Tag workflow id>>>
  namespace: <<<Tag namespace>>>
status:
  jobCount: <<<Tag related jobs count>>>
  testCaseCount: <<<Tag related test cases count>>>
  testCaseStatusSummary: <<<Tag test case count by status>>>
    success: N
    failure: N
    error: N
    skipped: N
    cancelled: N
```

functions.name: opentf.commons.datasources._collect_job_times_and_id
documentation:
Collect job start and end time, if available.

# Required parameters

- events: a list of events
- request_metadata: the channel request metadata for the job

# Returned object

A dictionary with the following entries:

- job_id
- requestTime

If the job started, it contains the additional entries:

- startTime
- endTime
- duration
# Datasource: Jobs

functions.name: opentf.commons.datasources._make_job_datasource
documentation:
Make datasource object for job.

# Required parameters

- job_name: a string, the 'short' job name
- request_metadata: the channel request metadata for the job or {}
- job: a dictionary, the job definition
- parent: a workflow or a generatorresult event
- events: a list of events

# Returned value

A 'Job' datasource object.

functions.name: opentf.commons.datasources.get_jobs
documentation:
Extract metadata for each job.

# Required parameters:

- events: a list of events

# Returned value:

A dictionary. Keys are job names, values are dictionaries with
job metadata, spec, and status.

`jobs_testcases` is a dictionary of entries like:

```
apiVersion: opentestfactory.org/v1alpha1
kind: Job
metadata:
  name: <<<Job name>>
  id: <<<Job uuid>>>
  namespace: <<<Job namespace>>>
  workflow_id: <<<Job workflow id>>>
  creationTimestamp: <<<Job creation timestamp>>>
spec:
  runs-on: <<<Job execution environment tags>>>
  variables: <<<Workflow and job specific environment variables>>>
status:
  phase: <<<Job phase>>>
  requestTime: <<<Job execution environment request time>>>
  startTime: <<<Job start time>>>
  endTime: <<<Job end time>>>
  duration: <<<Job duration (endTime - startTime)>>>
  testCaseCount: <<<Job test case count>>>
  testCaseStatusSummary: <<<Job test case count by status>>>
    success: N
    failure: N
    error: N
    skipped: N
    cancelled: N
```

classes.name: opentf.commons.datasources.DataSourceScopeError
documentation: DataSourceScopeError class
# Helpers

classes.name: opentf.commons.datasources.DataSourceDataError
documentation: DataSourceDataError class
